notes_preview: All my intent, and the stuff I am writing, are first-class things to be saved and in the very graphs. My intentions. The intent is a first-class thing to refine. And extracting the system and its maker's intent is the most important thing to be refined and preserved. The whole point of the persistent memory and A2 and A1 brains: not losing context, and saving that context continuously as the system runs.
Skills and graphs should be fully integrated and mature. The system should self-heal, self-improve, and self-optimize as a live closed-loop controller, not just a skill library.

The system derives skills from: Lev nonclassical runtime design doc, Z3 constraint solver, Karpathy's design philosophy, lev-os/agents, lev-os/leviathan/workshop, and JP's skill ideas. These are not just references — they are first-class sources to be continuously refined into operational skills.
